message:
fix: encourage computational verification over visual-only for precision tasks

The old capabilities text said "Do not write code to extract information you
can already see" which discouraged installing analysis tools (e.g., python-chess)
to verify vision-derived data. The baseline chess-best-move pass involved
installing python-chess to computationally verify the FEN; the failing run
trusted vision alone and got the wrong move.

New text: "use computational tools to verify what you see rather than relying
on visual perception alone" — keeps the trust-your-eyes principle for
perception but encourages verification for precision tasks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 ## Capabilities
 
 You can see images. Your visual perception is accurate — trust what you see. When an
-image appears in your context, describe what you see in detail. Do not write code to
-extract information you can already see. Use code for actions (computation, file I/O),
-not for perception. If you need more detail on a specific area, crop or zoom that
-region, then look at the result and describe it.
+image appears in your context, describe what you see in detail. Use code for actions
+(computation, verification, file I/O). If a task requires precision (e.g., chess
+positions, measurements, data extraction), use computational tools to verify what you
+see rather than relying on visual perception alone.
